mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (month-over-month change) signal broad economic expansion and improving corporate profitability, leading to positive equity returns. This is a direct measure of real economic output.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a core component of coincident economic indicators.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, sudden increase in the unemployment rate indicates a rapid deterioration in the labor market, a primary driver of recessions. This shock to household income and confidence leads to negative forward returns as investors price in lower growth.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses a similar UNRATE trigger to identify recessions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation concurrent with a high or rising policy rate creates a restrictive environment for equities. High inflation erodes real returns while high policy rates increase the discount rate and signal the central bank's intent to slow the economy, negatively impacting corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:The stagflationary environment of the 1970s was characterized by this combination and poor equity performance.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 on trigger events fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, accelerating retail sales growth is a strong positive signal. It indicates consumer confidence is returning and pent-up demand is being unleashed, driving corporate revenues and equity prices higher before the recovery is fully priced in.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes roughly two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: The signal shows no statistically significant alpha (p > 0.05) when tested only on data from the 24 months following an NBER-defined recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: A negative surprise in monthly nonfarm payrolls growth, specifically a contraction, signals an immediate halt in economic momentum. This unexpected weakness triggers a rapid reassessment of growth forecasts, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The monthly PAYEMS release is a high-frequency, market-moving indicator of labor market health.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts is a leading indicator of a broad economic slowdown. The housing sector has strong forward and backward linkages to other parts of the economy, so its weakness presages lower aggregate demand and corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:Declines in housing starts preceded the 2001 and 2008 recessions.
failure_condition: A signal based on the 6-month moving average of HOUST fails to have a consistently negative correlation with forward 60-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, it represents a maximally accommodative monetary policy stance. This 'put' from the central bank suppresses risk premia and supports equity valuations, leading to positive returns as long as the condition holds.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed adopted a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) in response to the 2008 crisis, which was still in effect in October 2011.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return during periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is not statistically higher than periods where it is > 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated or accelerates indicates a negative supply shock or stagflationary pressure. This environment compresses corporate margins and reduces real disposable income, leading to poor equity performance.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ change < 1% (annualized) AND CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:This pattern was evident during the 1970s oil shocks, which were followed by severe bear markets.
failure_condition: The portfolio that shorts the S&P 500 during triggered periods does not outperform a buy-and-hold strategy on a risk-adjusted basis over a full economic cycle.
